mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue momentum for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns, with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of the federal funds rate below 0.25% identifies an extreme liquidity regime in which discount-rate support and search-for-yield flows lift equities over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% after being at or above 0.25% within the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have a median 60-day S&P 500 return less than or equal to the unconditional median, or if the positive hit rate is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad labor-market improvement, raising expected aggregate income and earnings, and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition fails to outperform cases where only one condition holds, or if triggered 20-day returns are positive in 50% or fewer observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid CPI growth while the policy rate remains near zero signals an inflation shock not yet offset by monetary tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have average 20-day S&P 500 returns above zero, or if adding the FEDFUNDS interaction does not improve out-of-sample classification accuracy versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production signals weakening real output and margin pressure in cyclical sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after release.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls more than 0.5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 3-month declines greater than 0.5% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing above 1500 thousand units signals strong construction demand and durable-goods spillovers, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above 1500 after being at or below 1500 within the prior 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST crossings above 1500 do not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered periods, or if the return spread is negative over two consecutive walk-forward years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals deteriorating household income and recession risk, causing investors to mark down expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if downside hit rate is below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP paired with expanding industrial production identifies synchronized activity growth, increasing expected corporate cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly change > 0 and INDPRO 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-up and INDPRO-up condition has a 60-day return mean no greater than the unconditional mean, or if either proxy alone fully subsumes the interaction in walk-forward tests.
